mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 200k signals robust labor market tightening, which increases inflation expectations and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield upward as investors demand higher real returns. This mechanism operates continuously as a trend signal.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2014 payroll growth at 138252k suggests continued labor market recovery
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in > 55% of 20-day periods when monthly PAYEMS growth exceeds 200k, or Rank IC < 0.05 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains near zero (below 0.25%) while inflation-adjusted real rates are deeply negative, the yield curve steepens as investors rotate into longer-duration assets, driving 10-year yields higher as the Fed signals eventual normalization.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2014 FEDFUNDS at 0.09% with CPIAUCSL at 236.254
regime_hint: zero lower bound with emerging inflation expectations
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls in > 50% of triggered instances over 60-day horizons, or the interaction coefficient between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts below 900k units signals weakening demand momentum and recession risk, causing investors to flee to safety and driving 10-year Treasury yields downward as flight-to-quality accelerates.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 900k or declines > 10% month-over-month
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 55% of 20-day periods following HOUST declines below 900k, or Rank IC > -0.03 (fails to show negative correlation).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 3% annualized signals strong supply-side capacity utilization and inflation pressure, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise as the market prices in higher future inflation and Fed tightening.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2014 INDPRO at 102.7435, showing continued recovery
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in > 50% of 5-day forward periods when INDPRO growth is positive, or Rank IC < 0.04 over rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth combined with housing starts above 950k indicates strong aggregate demand, which raises inflation expectations and pushes 10-year Treasury yields higher as the market anticipates Fed rate normalization.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2014 RSAFS at 434571M and HOUST at 946k
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in > 55% of 20-day periods when both conditions are met, or the joint interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 6.5% signals labor market deterioration and recession risk, triggering a flight-to-quality rotation that drives 10-year Treasury yields downward as investors seek safe havens.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points in 1 month
evidence_refs: fact:May 2014 UNRATE at 6.3%, near historical threshold
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early-cycle weakness
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 50% of 20-day periods following UNRATE increases > 0.3pp, or Rank IC > -0.04 (fails to show negative correlation).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerating above 2.5% annualized combined with inflation-adjusted real rates remaining negative creates a regime where the Fed must normalize policy, pushing 10-year Treasury yields higher as the market prices in future rate increases.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 2.5% annualized AND FEDFUNDS < 0.5%
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with policy normalization pressure
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in > 55% of 60-day periods when both conditions are triggered, or the three-way interaction is not statistically significant (p > 0.20).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in real retail sales (RSAFS adjusted for CPIAUCSL) below the 6-month moving average signals demand weakness and deflationary pressure, causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall as investors anticipate slower growth and lower inflation.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS real growth (month-over-month) < 0% for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:May 2014 RSAFS at 434571M with CPIAUCSL at 236.254
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 50% of 60-day periods following sustained real retail sales declines, or Rank IC > -0.05 (fails to show negative correlation).
